compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
Framework seam: make the agent's structured-output **contract pluggable per operation**, and add the `completion_verdict` contract + schema. The runner currently hardcodes `findings_response_model()`; this is the enabling change (epic req #4).

## Scope (files)
- `src/rebar/llm/contracts.py` (NEW): name→response-model registry; `response_model_for(output_schema)` (default findings); import-clean (pydantic only inside builders); `completion_verdict_response_model` defined here.
- `src/rebar/llm/findings.py`: extract `citation_model()`/`finding_model()` lazy builders (shared Citation); promote `_validate_structured`→public `validate_structured`; **BI-1**: `finalize_outcome(mode="structured")` uses `model_dump(exclude_none=True)`.
- `src/rebar/llm/runner.py`: both runners select response model via `contracts.response_model_for(req.output_schema)` (lazy import inside run()); add `RunRequest.extra_tools` appended to the tool list (name-collision check).
- `src/rebar/schemas/completion_verdict.schema.json` (NEW, full-URL `$id`) + register `COMPLETION_VERDICT`/`OUTPUT_SCHEMAS["verify_completion"]`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `response_model_for(None)`→findings; `("completion_verdict")`→CompletionVerdict; unknown→findings default
- [ ] schema-pin test: CompletionVerdict mirrors `completion_verdict` schema; existing findings pin still passes
- [ ] BI-1: a model with omitted optionals serializes without nulls and validates (real-model test, not FakeRunner)
- [ ] `import rebar.llm` pulls no pydantic (import-cleanliness); existing review/code/scan ops unchanged
- [ ] `extra_tools` appended in both runners with a duplicate-name guard
compiled_state.title: WS-1: Pluggable output contract + completion_verdict schema (+ BI-1)
